title: George Clooney Defends Jimmy Kimmel After Trump’s ‘Heated’ Call for His Firing: ‘Jokes Are Jokes’
content:
ByAntonio Ferme

George Clooneyis speaking out in defense ofJimmy KimmelafterDonald Trump’slatest attemptto pressure ABC to fire the late-night host. The president blasted Kimmel’s “despicable call to violence” on Monday, referring toa joke about Melaniahaving the glow of an “expectant widow.” The comment was made during a skit on “Jimmy Kimmel Live!” that aired two days before the White House Correspondents Dinner, which was ultimately canceled aftera live shooter infiltrated the building.

Clooney compared the backlash to another pre-dinner remark that was taken out of context online: one made by White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt.

“Jimmy’s a comedian, and I would argue that Karoline Leavitt didn’t mean shots should be fired,” Clooney said Monday night at the 51st Chaplin Award Gala, where he was honored for his contributions to cinema. “She was making a joke. Fair enough. You look at that side and go, ’Well, jokes are jokes.’ But the rhetoric is a little dangerous. And we’ve seen it a lot lately.”

LeavitttoldFox News on the red carpet before heading into the White House Correspondents Dinner that “there will be some shots fired tonight in the room,” presumably referring to the jokes written for the reception. Clooney says this kind of hyperbolic rhetoric “can be toned down.”

“When one side is calling anyone they disagree with traitors to the country, which is a charge that’s punishable by death, just because they don’t agree with someone, I think the rhetoric is a little too heated,” Clooney said.

The Chaplin Award Gala has previously recognized Hollywood figures who have been outspoken about current political events, including Pedro Almodóvar, Spike Lee andRobert Redford. Clooney, son of a journalist, has frequently advocated for freedom of the press. One of the actor’smost recent projectswas a stage adaptation of his 2005 film “Good Night, and Good Luck,” which chronicles Edward R. Murrow’s televised takedown of Senator Joseph McCarthy at the height of the Red Scare.

“My father’s rule when we were growing up was to challenge people with more power,” Clooney said. “That’s always been our mantra. That’s what my sister and I believed, and what my mother and father believed in. It’s served us well. It’s gotten us in trouble, and that’s a good thing.”

The evening’s gala presenters includedStephen Colbert, whose Late Show debut in 2015 featured Clooney as his first guest, along with several of his co-stars from various points in his career, including Julianna Margulies (“ER”), Sam Rockwell (“Confessions of a Dangerous Mind”) and John Turturro (“Brother, Where Art Thou?”).

“I always have faith in the press. I always believe in it. I’m the son of a journalist, you know?“ Clooney concluded. “I also think that you’re not supposed to have a good relationship with government. You’re supposed to be questioning them. The people that I support- the Democrats. So, I’m a Democrat. I make no apologies for it. The people that I support — Bill Clinton, Barack Obama — they didn’t enjoy getting chased down by reporters. People in power don’t like to have to answer all the questions in the world. That’s fair. They shouldn’t enjoy it. And the reporters should make sure that they hold people’s feet to the fire. That’s the rules. That’s what Jefferson talked about in 1787. We need to be able to have a free and fair press.”
title_zh: 喬治·克隆尼(George Clooney)為吉米·基梅爾(Jimmy Kimmel)辯護，回應特朗普(Trump)對其“激烈”要求解雇的呼籲，表示：“笑話就是笑話。”
content_zh:
作者：安東尼奧·費爾梅 (Antonio Ferme)

喬治·克隆尼 (George Clooney) 在唐納德·川普 (Donald Trump) 最新試圖施壓 ABC 解雇深夜節目主持人吉米·基梅爾 (Jimmy Kimmel) 之後發聲為其辯護。總統在週一抨擊基梅爾的「可惡的暴力呼籲」，指的是一個關於梅蘭妮亞 (Melania) 擁有「期待的寡婦」光環的玩笑。這個評論是在「吉米·基梅爾直播秀！」（Jimmy Kimmel Live!）的一個小品中提出的，該小品在白宮記者晚宴前兩天播出，最終因一名槍手闖入大樓而被取消。

克隆尼將這一反彈與另一個在晚宴前被網上斷章取義的評論進行了比較：白宮新聞秘書卡羅琳·利維特 (Karoline Leavitt) 的言論。

克隆尼在第51屆查普林獎頒獎晚會上表示：「吉米是一名喜劇演員，我認為卡羅琳·利維特並不是說應該開槍。」他因對電影的貢獻而獲得了榮譽。「她只是在開玩笑。這是公平的。你看看那一邊，會說，‘玩笑就是玩笑。’但這種言辭有點危險。我們最近已經看到很多這樣的情況。」

利維特在進入白宮記者晚宴之前告訴福克斯新聞 (Fox News)：「今晚房間裡會有一些槍聲發出」，顯然是指為接待會寫的笑話。克隆尼表示，這種誇張的言辭「可以被緩和」。

「當一方稱任何與其意見不合的人為國家的叛徒，這是一項可判死刑的指控，僅僅因為他們不同意某人的觀點，我認為這種言辭有點過於激烈。」克隆尼說。

查普林獎頒獎晚會之前曾表彰過一些對當前政治事件直言不諱的好萊塢人物，包括佩德羅·阿莫多瓦 (Pedro Almodóvar)、斯派克·李 (Spike Lee) 和羅伯特·雷德福 (Robert Redford)。克隆尼作為一名記者的兒子，經常倡導新聞自由。這位演員最近的一個項目是他2005年電影《再見，祝你好運》（Good Night, and Good Luck）的舞台改編，該片記錄了愛德華·R·穆羅 (Edward R. Murrow) 在紅色恐慌高峰期對參議員約瑟夫·麥卡錫 (Joseph McCarthy) 的電視揭露。

克隆尼說：「我父親在我們成長過程中的規則是挑戰擁有更多權力的人。這一直是我們的座右銘。我和我妹妹相信這一點，我的母親和父親也相信。這對我們有很大幫助。這讓我們陷入麻煩，但這是好事。」

當晚的頒獎嘉賓包括斯蒂芬·科爾伯特 (Stephen Colbert)，他在2015年的《深夜秀》（Late Show）首秀中以克隆尼為首位嘉賓，還有他職業生涯中幾位合作演員，包括朱莉安娜·馬古利斯 (Julianna Margulies)（《急診室的春天》（ER））、山姆·洛克威爾 (Sam Rockwell)（《危險心靈的自白》（Confessions of a Dangerous Mind））和約翰·圖爾特羅 (John Turturro)（《兄弟，你在哪裡？》（Brother, Where Art Thou?））。

「我一直對媒體抱有信心。我始終相信它。我是記者的兒子，你知道嗎？」克魯尼(George Clooney)總結道。「我也認為，媒體不應該與政府保持良好的關係。你應該質疑他們。我支持的人——民主黨人。所以，我是一名民主黨人。我對此不感到抱歉。我支持的人——比爾·克林頓(Bill Clinton)、巴拉克·奧巴馬(Barack Obama)——他們並不喜歡被記者追著跑。掌權者不喜歡回答所有的問題。這是公平的。他們不應該享受這樣的情況。而記者應該確保他們讓人們面對現實。這就是規則。這就是傑佛遜(Thomas Jefferson)在1787年所談論的。我們需要能夠擁有一個自由和公正的媒體。」
summary_zh:
作者：安東尼奧·費爾梅 (Antonio Ferme)

喬治·克隆尼 (George Clooney) 在唐納德·川普 (Donald Trump) 最新試圖施壓 ABC 解雇深夜節目主持人吉米·基梅爾 (Jimmy Kimmel) 之後發聲為其辯護。總統在週一抨擊基梅爾的「可惡的暴力呼籲」，指的是一個關於梅蘭妮亞 (Melania) 擁有「期待的寡婦」光環的玩笑。
